message:
fix(scripts): check_compliance_matrices.sh runs all generators on failure (14rm)

Self-review on commit 01e8f1f found that the for-loop iterating the
three generators aborted at the first exit-1 (e.g. MUST < 95% gate).
Result: when multiple subtrees drift in the same PR, only the first
failure surfaced — engineers had to fix and rerun to see the rest.

Fix: collect generator exit codes in an array, keep iterating, then
combine with drift detection at the end. Single non-zero exit reflects
ANY failure across all 3 generators + drift checks; engineers see the
full surface picture in one CI run.

br-franken_numpy-14rm

+{"id":"franken_numpy-14rm","title":"[BUG] check_compliance_matrices.sh aborts on first generator failure","description":"scripts/check_compliance_matrices.sh (commit 01e8f1f) iterates the
three generators in a for-loop with `set -e`. If generator N fails
its own gate (e.g. MUST coverage < 95%), the loop aborts and the
remaining generators never run. Result: partial visibility into
matrix regressions when multiple subtrees drift in the same PR.

Self-review on commit 01e8f1f, found 2026-04-25.

Fix: collect generator exit codes + any drift in a flag variable,
keep running, then surface a combined failure at the end. Engineers
investigating a regression see the full surface picture in one run.

Acceptance:
- All 3 generators run regardless of intermediate exit codes.
- Final exit reflects ANY failure (gate or drift).
- The diff dump still shows per-output drift so the engineer can
  attribute regressions correctly.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T19:54:41.350879568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T19:55:01.702253265Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Include oracle test covering: positive/negative float scalars, 1-D array with mixed signs, integer array promoted to float, NaN preservation, +inf/-inf handling, and 2-D array.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:08:42.561431399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:20:46Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T08:20:46Z","close_reason":"Completed br-franken_numpy-29r9: numpy.fabs parity was already present on main","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"franken_numpy-2axo","title":"[BUG] testing_assert_string_equal lines_keepends incomplete separator set","description":"testing_assert_string_equal native port (commit dc2fcb6) added a free
fn lines_keepends in fnp-python/src/lib.rs that mirrors Python's
str.splitlines(keepends=True) for \n, \r, \r\n only. Python actually
recognizes a wider separator set: \v (\x0b), \f (\x0c), \x1c, \x1d,
\x1e, \x85,  ,  .

Self-review found in last-3-commits sweep on 2026-04-25.

For typical test input (newline-terminated lines) the divergence is
invisible, but a string containing e.g. \v will tokenise differently
than numpy's reference and produce a non-matching diff message,
breaking the exact-format parity guarantee documented in the commit.

Fix: drop lines_keepends and route through Python's stdlib via
`actual.into_pyobject(py)?.call_method1("splitlines", (true,))?` so
the canonical separator set is used. Cleaner, smaller, and correct.

Acceptance:
- lines_keepends helper deleted.
- testing_assert_string_equal calls actual.splitlines(True) /
  desired.splitlines(True) via pyo3.
- Existing testing_assert_string_equal parity test passes.
- Optional: extra parity oracle exercising \v and \r-only inputs to
  prove the broader separator set is honored (gated on
  numpy_available).","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T19:46:24.338509071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T19:46:46.112521028Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_numpy-2axo","title":"[BUG] testing_assert_string_equal lines_keepends incomplete separator set","description":"testing_assert_string_equal native port (commit dc2fcb6) added a free
fn lines_keepends in fnp-python/src/lib.rs that mirrors Python's
str.splitlines(keepends=True) for \n, \r, \r\n only. Python actually
recognizes a wider separator set: \v (\x0b), \f (\x0c), \x1c, \x1d,
\x1e, \x85,  ,  .

Self-review found in last-3-commits sweep on 2026-04-25.

For typical test input (newline-terminated lines) the divergence is
invisible, but a string containing e.g. \v will tokenise differently
than numpy's reference and produce a non-matching diff message,
breaking the exact-format parity guarantee documented in the commit.

Fix: drop lines_keepends and route through Python's stdlib via
`actual.into_pyobject(py)?.call_method1("splitlines", (true,))?` so
the canonical separator set is used. Cleaner, smaller, and correct.

Acceptance:
- lines_keepends helper deleted.
- testing_assert_string_equal calls actual.splitlines(True) /
  desired.splitlines(True) via pyo3.
- Existing testing_assert_string_equal parity test passes.
- Optional: extra parity oracle exercising \v and \r-only inputs to
  prove the broader separator set is honored (gated on
  numpy_available).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T19:46:24.338509071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T19:51:04.490115501Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T19:51:04.487575511Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

More complex than fixture-only work - deferring to avoid lib.rs conflicts with other agents.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","parity"]}
+{"id":"franken_numpy-tg33","title":"[FEAT] golden-snapshot regression gate for compliance matrix generators","description":"The three compliance matrix generators (fnp_python_compliance_matrix.py,
fnp_random_compliance_matrix.py, fnp_polynomial_compliance_matrix.py)
emit Markdown reports under crates/fnp-python/*COMPLIANCE.generated.md
but have no regression gate. If a future edit removes a #[pyfunction]
or a setattr on a submodule, the next regen produces a smaller matrix
silently — coverage drops are invisible until someone notices.

Per /testing-golden-artifacts skill: freeze the current matrix outputs
as committed-tracked golden files. Add a tiny shell script
scripts/check_compliance_matrices.sh that runs all three generators
and exits non-zero if any output has changed (git diff --quiet on the
*.generated.md). CI invokes it; engineers regen + review on intentional
changes.

Acceptance:
- scripts/check_compliance_matrices.sh exists, runs all 3 generators,
  diffs the outputs against tracked snapshots, exits 0 on no-change /
  exits 1 with a diff dump on regression.
- The committed *.generated.md files act as the golden snapshot.
- README or AGENTS.md notes when to regenerate (intentional surface
  changes only).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T19:52:06.213360738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T19:53:33.341624232Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T19:53:33.338602450Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-tgaq","title":"PORT ma: core MaskedArray classes/constants and predicate helper parity","description":"Reality-check against numpy.ma.__all__: fnp_python.ma currently exposes many function wrappers but not the core class/constant and predicate surface users import from numpy.ma. Add nested fnp_python.ma exports for MaskedArray, masked_array/array/asarray/asanyarray aliases as appropriate, masked/nomask/masked_print_option, MAError/MaskError, and predicate/data helpers getdata, is_mask, isMA, isMaskedArray, isarray, harden_mask, soften_mask. Include oracle tests for identity/type parity, masked singleton/nomask behavior, class construction, predicate truth tables, and error surfaces matching numpy.ma.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-numpy","created_at":"2026-04-25T03:43:57.872839608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:52:04.837361802Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T03:52:04.835562758Z","close_reason":"Completed in 7aab7f3","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-tgd","title":"Verify fnp-dtype promotion and cast policy surface","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:37:03.266261921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:38:43.424693377Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:38:43.424436842Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-thdj","title":"Add mode parameter to convolve/correlate ('full'/'valid'/'same')","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:56:44.641438509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T20:05:11.853167354Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T20:05:11.852771304Z","close_reason":"Added mode parameter ('full'/'valid'/'same') to convolve_mode and correlate_mode. Original convolve/correlate remain as 'full' wrappers. Added 6 tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -43,13 +43,15 @@ for output in "${OUTPUTS[@]}"; do
     fi
 done
 
-# Run all three generators. They write their outputs in place. If any
-# generator exits non-zero on its own gate (e.g. MUST < 95%), surface
-# that exit too — that's a regression we want to flag.
+# 14rm: run all three generators regardless of any one's exit code so
+# engineers see the full surface picture in a single run. Per-generator
+# gate failures (MUST < 95%) and drift events both contribute to a
+# unified non-zero exit at the end.
+generator_failures=()
 for gen in "${GENERATORS[@]}"; do
     if ! python3 "$gen"; then
-        echo "FAIL: $gen exited non-zero (likely MUST coverage below threshold)" >&2
-        exit 1
+        generator_failures+=("$gen")
+        echo "FAIL: $gen exited non-zero (gate or runtime error)" >&2
     fi
 done
 
@@ -68,7 +70,15 @@ for output in "${OUTPUTS[@]}"; do
     fi
 done
 
-if [[ $drift -ne 0 ]]; then
+if [[ ${#generator_failures[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+    echo "" >&2
+    echo "Generator(s) exited non-zero (likely MUST < 95% gate):" >&2
+    for gen in "${generator_failures[@]}"; do
+        echo "  $gen" >&2
+    done
+fi
+
+if [[ ${#generator_failures[@]} -gt 0 || $drift -ne 0 ]]; then
     echo "" >&2
     echo "Compliance matrix regression detected. If the surface change is" >&2
     echo "intentional, regenerate and commit the snapshot:" >&2
